figures.caption: Figure 1 The MoFA pipeline with Divide-and-Conquer scaling. The process begins by partitioning the candidate pool into disjoint buckets (Phase 1). An LLM agent (Φθ) performing sequential reasoning first selects local candidate subsets from each bucket. These subsets are merged into a refined candidate pool, where the agent performs a final round of global sequential selection (Phase 2) to reach the target size K.
Table 1 Prediction results for true interest and time-worthiness tasks. Groups denotes the number of distinct feature groups in the selected subset; Train and Test refer to AUC scores.
